title: ophrys argolica subsp. lesbis
intro: Ophrys argolica subsp. lesbis, also known as the Lesvos bee orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid found in Greece. This plant is a subspecies of the Ophrys argolica orchid and is characterized by its unique flowers that mimic the appearance and scent of female bees, which attracts male bees for pollination. The Lesvos bee orchid has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been reported to have medicinal properties that aid in wound healing and reducing fever. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments that are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
